rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Intervene In This Case And Seek An Order To Enforce The Parties Settlement By Prospective Intervenors Trn Law Associates And Tiffany R. Norman
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2022 line 1. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO INTERVENE IN THIS CASE AND SEEK AN ORDER TO ENFORCE THE PARTIES SETTLEMENT BY PROSPECTIVE INTERVENORS TRN LAW ASSOCIATES AND TIFFANY R. NORMAN is GRANTED, no opposition filed. Complaint in intervention to be filed and served forthwith. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VIRGIL DENISE BRADLEY-TYSON VS. DWIGHT W. DAVENPORT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Striking Certain Requests For Production Of Documents To Defendant Herb Thomas Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER STRIKING CERTAIN REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS TO DEFENDANT HERB THOMAS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is denied as to Requests 1-5, 7, 8, 16 and 17. Motion is otherwise GRANTED as follows: Thomas shall provide a verified written response to RFP, Set One within 30 days of notice of entry of order. As for the responsive documents, THOMAS is to Bates number all the documents and produce them only to counsel for BRADLEY-TYSON, who will have seven (7) court days after receipt to prepare and serve a designation with sub-categories of the Bates-numbered pages being withheld from production, a basic description of the type of those document(s), along one or more of the following grounds they are asserting: privacy, unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment and oppression; and/or do not lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. At the same time BRADLEY-TYSON serves the designation of withheld documents, she must serve the remaining Bates-numbered documents that were designated and withheld. Counsel for KELLER WILLIAMS and BRADLEY-TYSON shall meet & confer in person or by phone, over any withheld documents. If any issues remain in dispute parties may file a new motion addressing the issues. Request for sanctions is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2022 line 6. HEARING ON JOHN DEDELLA'S CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION Hearing Required.
